title: Chicken au Curried Poivre
summary: A classic peppercorn sauce, but with even more flavour. This curried twist makes for a seriously rich and satisfying dish. Best served with a tangy salad to cut through the richness and some crusty bread to mop up every drop of sauce.

method.description: Heat the oven to 200°C. Coarsely grind or bash the peppercorns. Make sure your butter is fridge-cold.

Generously season the chicken legs with salt. Place them skin side down into an ovenproof frying pan over a medium heat. Cook undisturbed until the skin is golden brown, 8-12 mins. Transfer the chicken onto a plate, skin side up – it won’t be cooked through yet. Don’t wash out the pan.

Finely slice the parsley stems, setting aside the leaves for later. Add the stems to the empty pan along with the crushed peppercorns, crispy onions and curry powder. Set it over a medium heat and cook for 30 secs, stirring, until fragrant.

Pour in the vinegar (for the sauce) and water, then nestle the chicken back in, skin side up. Bring to the boil, then transfer the pan to the oven. Roast for 30-35 mins until the chicken is cooked through.
Meanwhile, for the salad, whisk together the Dijon mustard, vinegar and olive oil until emulsified. Taste and season with salt and black pepper.

Once the chicken is cooked, transfer it to a plate to rest. Place the pan back over a medium heat. Stir in the crème fraîche, cold butter and miso. Let the sauce reduce if you’d like it thicker. Taste and adjust with more salt, pepper or vinegar if needed.
Toss the salad leaves, walnuts and reserved parsley leaves in the dressing. Divide between plates.
Serve the chicken with plenty of sauce and some crusty baguette on the side.

chefs.name: Chloe René
chefs.summary: Chloe (@chlo_eat) takes inspiration from her diverse upbringing to create recipes and dishes influenced from cultures around the world. Despite her classical training, Chloe's philosophy in the kitchen revolves around simplicity and home-cooking. She firmly believes that the essence of a great meal lies in the quality of ingredients and the care put into their preparation. Instead of relying on complex and technical processes, Chloe prizes the art of letting ingredients shine through, enhancing their natural flavours with a delicate touch.
